items.title: Align Element Plus Control Sizes in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update removes the custom size overrides for Element Plus components, ensuring that `el-input`, `el-select`, and `el-button` all align at the same height. The `_common.scss` file has been modified to revert to the native sizes provided by Element Plus, enhancing consistency across the UI. Additionally, the dependency on `@acedatacloud/core` has been bumped to version `^0.17.0` to reflect these changes.

items.title: Standardize Element Plus Control Sizes in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update removes custom size overrides for Element Plus components, aligning all controls to their native sizes. The changes ensure that `el-input`, `el-button`, and `el-select` now share consistent dimensions, enhancing UI coherence. Additionally, the dependency on `@acedatacloud/core` has been bumped to version 0.17.0 to reflect these adjustments.

items.title: Enhance Kling CLI with New Image List Feature in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `--image-list` option to the Kling image-to-video CLI, allowing users to provide a structured JSON array of images. The change maintains backward compatibility with existing options while improving the request schema to align with the latest documentation. Additionally, focused tests have been added to ensure the new functionality works as intended.

items.title: Enhance Multi-Image Support for Kling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `image_list` parameter for the `kling-video-o1` model, allowing users to reference multiple images in their requests. The OpenAPI schema and documentation have been updated accordingly, including examples and usage guidelines. Note that the new functionality will only be effective after the deployment of the related PlatformService PR.

items.title: Enhance Kling Video Handler to Support Image List in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update allows the `kling-video-o1` worker to accept and forward an optional `image_list` from the request body, enhancing its functionality. The changes include reading the `image_list`, appending it to the existing frame arrays, and improving logging for better traceability. This ensures that the worker can now handle multiple image references effectively, aligning with the official structure.

items.title: Fix control sizes for consistency in Common Frontend 🎨
items.summary: This update resolves inconsistencies in Element Plus control sizes within the settings pop-up. The shared control adapter no longer overrides sizes, ensuring that buttons and inputs align with Element Plus defaults. The version has been updated from 0.16.0 to 0.17.0, and tests have been adjusted to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance resilience of createTask with retries in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the resilience of the createTask function by increasing the HTTP timeout from 30 seconds to 60 seconds and implementing a retry mechanism for transient errors. The changes ensure that a single cross-border submit does not fail the entire request, while deterministic errors are still handled promptly. Overall, this enhancement aims to reduce customer-visible failures on the default route.

items.title: Implement White-Label SMS Webhook Support in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a white-label SMS webhook feature that allows per-site configurations via a read-only connection to the PlatformBackend. Key changes include the addition of a new `SiteConfig` model, modifications to the SMS delivery service, and the exposure of a new `/sms-code` endpoint. The implementation ensures that AuthBackend remains site-agnostic while securely delivering SMS codes to specified webhooks.

items.title: Enhance SMS Webhook Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a per-site white-label SMS webhook configuration under the existing Site.auth structure. Key changes include the addition of a write-only secret for the webhook, ensuring it is never exposed in GET requests, and the implementation of validation and redaction functions. The changes are fully tested with 18 new test cases, ensuring robust functionality without altering existing models or requiring migrations.

items.title: Update element_id to integer type for CLI commands in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the CLI to align with the OpenAPI specification by changing the `element_id` type from string to integer for the `POST /kling/videos` endpoint. Key changes include updating the `--element-id` option to enforce integer validation and adjusting the internal data structures accordingly. Tests have also been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring that numeric values are used consistently.

items.title: Enhance SMS Code Requests with White-Label Site Origin in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to send the white-label site origin with SMS code requests. The changes include modifications to the SMS code operator to inject the site parameter from the login query, ensuring that SMS delivery is routed to the appropriate subsite webhook. This enhancement maintains existing functionality for the main site while improving flexibility for subsites.

items.title: Ensure @acedatacloud Scope Uses Public Registry in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `.npmrc` file to pin the `@acedatacloud` scope to the public npmjs registry. This change addresses issues with Element Plus component sizing on machines using outdated corporate proxies, ensuring that the latest version of `@acedatacloud/core` is always accessible. Existing installations will need to reinstall core to apply the fix.

items.title: Enhance Polling and Error Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces critical robustness improvements for the SeekAPI polling mechanism, allowing retries on transient network issues and timeouts. Additionally, it enhances error reporting by parsing HTTP 200 responses that contain error messages, ensuring clearer feedback for validation failures. These changes improve the overall reliability and user experience of the Platform Service.

items.title: New buyback entry added for July 2026 in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new on-chain buyback record to the Roadmap Buyback History. The transaction details include a swap of 26.776458 USDC for 66,308.084697 ACE, processed via the Jupiter Aggregator. All data has been verified for accuracy and conforms to the required schema.

items.title: Enhance Omni-Video Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the documentation for the omni-video reference and video-edit capabilities of the kling-video-o1 model. Key changes include the addition of item structures for video and element lists, clarification of prompt referencing conventions, and a new section detailing video editing examples. These enhancements aim to eliminate confusion and ensure users can effectively utilize the video editing features.

items.title: Ensure @acedatacloud Scope Points to Public npmjs in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `.npmrc` file to pin the `@acedatacloud/*` scope to the public npmjs registry. This change addresses issues where contributors using outdated corporate proxies were unable to access the latest version of `@acedatacloud/core`, resulting in missing styles for Element Plus controls. The fix ensures that future installations will not revert to older versions, maintaining consistent styling across all environments.

items.title: Remove focus outline from chat composer textarea in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update eliminates the unwanted 2px square focus outline from the chat composer textarea, which was caused by a global CSS rule. A specificity override has been implemented to ensure the textarea does not display the outline while maintaining accessibility through the wrapper's focus indication. The fix has been verified on the live page, confirming the outline is now removed as intended.

items.title: Introducing SeekAPI Worker for Enhanced Video Generation in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `seek` worker for the PlatformService, integrating the 优影云 SeekAPI for Doubao Seedance 2.0 video generation. Key features include real-person references, 4k support, and seamless integration with existing task databases, ensuring billing parity with the ephone channel. The deployment includes new endpoints and robust error handling for improved performance.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Apps with Semantic Icons in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update upgrades `@acedatacloud/core` to version `^0.16.0` and pins `@lucide/vue` to `1.23.0`. It migrates nine static OAuth Apps call sites to use explicit semantic icon subpaths while ensuring accessibility improvements, such as persistent native buttons for copy actions. Additionally, the localized `common.button.copy` contract has been added to all 18 locale catalogs.

items.title: Enhanced Semantic Icons in Shared Controls in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update upgrades `@acedatacloud/core` to version `0.16.0` and pins `@lucide/vue` to `1.23.0`. It migrates several static icon call sites to use explicit semantic subpaths while maintaining some icons with Font Awesome until further review. Accessibility improvements include converting the header search trigger to a native button and enhancing screen reader announcements for copy actions.

items.title: Upgrade to Semantic Icons in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the Scheduled Tasks by migrating four static actions to explicit semantic icon subpaths, improving accessibility and user experience. The core package has been upgraded to version 0.16.0, and the Lucide icons are now integrated for better semantic clarity. Additionally, necessary validation checks have been performed to ensure stability.

items.title: Enhance GitHub Proxy with Responses Shim in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a shim in the GitHub proxy that allows responses-only models to be accessed via the standard chat completions API. Key models like gpt-5.4, gpt-5.5, and gpt-5.6 are now fully supported, ensuring seamless integration while maintaining the functionality of the native responses endpoint. Comprehensive unit tests validate the implementation, enhancing reliability and performance.

items.title: Exciting new GPT-5.6 models added to CLIs in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update syncs the `aichat` and `openai` CLI packages with the latest GPT-5.6 model variants: `gpt-5.6-luna`, `gpt-5.6-terra`, and `gpt-5.6-sol`. These models have been added to the respective model lists, ensuring they are prominently featured at the top. This enhancement aligns with the OpenAPI specifications and improves user access to the latest capabilities.

items.title: Enhance China Billing Requirements in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update mandates that all China billing profiles must include essential invoice details such as invoice type, taxpayer ID, company address, company phone, bank name, and bank account. The billing profile serializer tests have been updated to ensure compliance with these requirements, enhancing the validation process for both general and special VAT invoices.

items.title: Exciting New GPT-5.6 Models Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three new OpenAI GPT-5.6 chat models: `gpt-5.6-luna`, `gpt-5.6-terra`, and `gpt-5.6-sol`. Key changes include updated billing rules, model visibility in chat completions, and comprehensive documentation updates. All models are now live and ready for use, enhancing our chat capabilities significantly.

items.title: Exciting New GPT-5.6 Models and Fixes in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for three new OpenAI GPT-5.6 models: `gpt-5.6-luna`, `gpt-5.6-terra`, and `gpt-5.6-sol`. Key changes include adding these models to the `VISIBLE_MODELS` list and implementing price-safe fallback chains. Additionally, regression tests have been added to ensure the new fallback logic is functioning correctly.

items.title: Enhance phone confirmation classification in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update classifies WeChat's phone-confirmation page as 'logging_in' to improve accuracy in user authentication states. It prevents misclassification by avoiding generic substring matching and ensures that visible text is preserved without reporting a percentage for this state. The changes have been validated with successful unit tests and a clean review.

items.title: Exciting New Semantic Icons Added in Common Frontend 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new semantic icon manifest with 12 tree-shakeable Lucide icons, enhancing our iconography while keeping brand logos separate. The core package has been bumped to version 0.16.0, and all changes have been validated through comprehensive testing. Additionally, documentation has been updated to reflect the new exact-pin requirements for consumers.

items.title: Refactor Design to Adopt Shared Control Adapter in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update upgrades the core to version 0.15.0 and loads the new `controls.css` after the app and Tailwind styles. It also removes 94 lines of duplicated global control SCSS while retaining essential features like the primary glow and mobile dialog layout. Comprehensive validation has been performed, ensuring a clean and efficient codebase.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Popup Recovery Mechanism in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of non-minimized WeChat windows by ensuring that the release-notes popup is correctly processed. Key changes include adjustments to the compact-window handler and the dismissal logic for minimized popups, enhancing overall reliability. Validation through unit tests confirms the effectiveness of these modifications.

items.title: Revamp SMS Delivery for White-Label Sites in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new design document for SMS delivery webhooks, allowing white-label sites to send verification messages without displaying the Nexior brand. Key changes include the removal of hardcoded signatures and the implementation of a per-site configuration, ensuring compliance and responsibility lies with the site owners. The document outlines security measures and operational constraints for SMS delivery in China.

items.title: Unified Login Experience with Floating Popup in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update enhances the native and desktop login experience by replacing the full-screen white page with a centered floating popup. The new design features a semi-transparent blurred background, a close button, and maintains the functionality of the previous implementation. All platforms now present a consistent login interface, improving user experience and accessibility.

items.title: Enhance Email Verification with Custom Logo in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update integrates the white-label site logo into the email verification process. The logo is now sent alongside the verification code in registration, password reset, and email binding functionalities. This change ensures that the branding is consistent with the current sub-site, enhancing user experience.

items.title: Enhance Email Verification with Custom Logos in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces support for custom white-label logos in verification emails, allowing sub-sites to display their own branding. The backend now accepts an optional `logo` parameter in the email code request, which is safely processed to prevent security risks. Additionally, nine new unit tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended while maintaining security standards.

items.title: Streamline 'View Code' Visibility in Nexior 🌐
items.summary: This update modifies the display logic for the 'View Code' button, ensuring it only appears on the main official host, `studio.acedata.cloud`. Redundant site configuration options have been removed, leading to a cleaner codebase with a net change of +17 lines added and -228 lines deleted. The changes enhance security by limiting API exposure on non-official domains.

items.title: Enhance Telegram Skill with Forum Topic Support in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces crucial support for forum topics in the Telegram skill, allowing users to list topics and send messages to specific threads. The new `list-topics` command enables users to view available topics, while the `send` command now accepts a `--topic` option for posting into those threads. These enhancements improve user interaction in forum-style supergroups, ensuring messages reach the right audience.

items.title: Refactor design to adopt shared control adapter in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update upgrades the core to version 0.15.0 and loads `controls.css` after the app styles. It eliminates 104 lines of duplicated SCSS for Button, Input, Select, Card, and Dialog adapters while retaining Auth-specific components. Validation has been completed with ESLint, vue-tsc, and builds, ensuring a clean and efficient codebase.

items.title: Refactor design to adopt shared control adapter in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update upgrades the core to version 0.15.0 and modifies the loading order of stylesheets in both CSR and SSR entries. It removes 97 lines of duplicated SCSS for Button, Input, Select, Card, and Dialog adapters while retaining specific Platform components like breadcrumbs and mobile-dialog layouts. The changes have been validated through various build checks and ensure no overflow issues in the mobile view.

items.title: Clean up application extras imports in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update formats the application extras route imports to ensure compliance with the repository-wide Prettier standards. Additionally, a patch change record has been added to document this modification. Importantly, this change does not affect any existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance QR Handling and Localization in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the QR request process by ensuring it only triggers for recognized WeChat states, reducing unnecessary retries. Additionally, it introduces localized messages for blocked, security, and login progress statuses, enhancing user experience across multiple languages. Stale QR task polling is now canceled when the flow stops or the page unmounts, ensuring better resource management.

items.title: Enhance QR Recovery Fail-Safe in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the QR recovery process by ensuring that navigation from unrecognized or transitional WeChat states is refused. Key changes include the implementation of explicit error responses for various states and the addition of comprehensive tests to cover logout behavior. Overall, these enhancements bolster the security and reliability of the authentication flow.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Window Detection and Diagnostics in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the detection and handling of WeChat compact windows by implementing a more robust arbitration mechanism. Key changes include enhanced UIA evidence requirements for login detection and the introduction of read-only diagnostics for window states. The modifications aim to ensure accurate identification of login states and improve overall system reliability.

items.title: Enhance Site Code Visibility Control in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a configurable option for the 'View Code' button visibility on a per-site basis. By default, the button will be hidden on white-label subsites while remaining visible on the official site. Administrators can now toggle this setting in the site configuration, improving control over API exposure.

items.title: Enhance Contact UI with Icons and Smaller Inputs in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update brings a fresh look to the Support Contacts feature in Site Settings. Each summary chip now displays an icon corresponding to the contact type, enhancing visual clarity. Additionally, editor controls have been resized to 'small', improving the overall user experience without altering any core functionality.

items.title: Enhance Core Control Adapter in Common Frontend 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new shared core control adapter, standardizing key UI components like Button, Input, and Card while preserving existing styles. The adapter's CSS is now imported after application styles to ensure proper styling precedence. Additionally, the core package version has been bumped to 0.15.0, enhancing overall functionality and stability.

items.title: Launch of the New Marketing Landing Page in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fully functional marketing landing page at `/home` for Nexior, showcasing our all-in-one AI capabilities across chat, image, music, and video. Key enhancements include a new route, improved layout with multiple sections, and internationalization support across 17 locales. The changes also fix previous navigation issues, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Introducing the BrowserDevice Bridge for aichat2 in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update proposes a new outbound-only BrowserDevice architecture for aichat2, eliminating public endpoints and cloud fallbacks. Key features include a dedicated Browser Relay, a first-class `browser_session` Connector mode, and enhanced local executor capabilities. The documentation outlines a staged migration plan and addresses various risks through thorough validation and adversarial reviews.

items.title: Enhance Contact Editor with Icons and Reliability in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two key improvements to the Support Contacts editor. Each contact row now displays the corresponding channel icon, enhancing visual clarity. Additionally, saved contacts will reliably appear upon reopening the editor, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance UI with P0 Interaction Baseline in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update upgrades the core to version 0.14.0 and implements a new mapping for Element control density at 32/40/48px. It also adopts shared tokens for keyboard focus, elevation, and motion, while preserving existing button variants and shadow modes. Comprehensive validation has been performed, ensuring a clean build and improved mobile responsiveness.

items.title: Enhanced Interaction Design in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update upgrades the core to version 0.14.0 and implements a new interaction baseline with improved control densities. Key changes include the adoption of shared tokens for keyboard focus, motion, and elevation, while preserving existing button variants and shadow modes. Validation checks confirm that all elements render correctly, ensuring a smooth user experience.

items.title: Enhanced Interaction Design in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a significant upgrade to core version 0.14.0, enhancing the Element control density for 32/40/48px. Key design tokens for keyboard focus, elevation, and motion have been adopted, while preserving existing button variants and shadow modes. The changes ensure a more cohesive user experience across the application.

items.title: Dynamic Brand Name in Welcome Toast for Nexior 🎉
items.summary: The welcome toast for new users now dynamically displays the site brand instead of the hardcoded 'Nexior'. This change ensures that white-label tenants see their own brand name, enhancing the user experience across different locales. The implementation updates the relevant i18n keys to use a placeholder for the brand name, ensuring consistency and safety in rendering.

items.title: Hide Crypto Surfaces for CN Visitors in Frontend Web 🚫
items.summary: This update introduces a geo-gating feature that hides crypto, ACE-coin, and x402 surfaces from users in mainland China. Key components affected include the homepage token carousel, top navigation menu, and console links, ensuring compliance with regional regulations. The implementation leverages a new `country` field from the backend to determine user location dynamically.

items.title: New Disable Recharge Toggle for Site Config in Nexior ⚡
items.summary: This update introduces a new toggle in the site configuration settings that allows administrators to disable recharge options for users. When activated, all recharge and purchase buttons will be hidden, preventing users from topping up their balances. This feature aims to support scenarios like offline settlements or internal use, providing greater control for site administrators.

items.title: Enhance Telegram Skill with Join/Leave Commands in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability for the Telegram skill to join and leave groups or channels, a crucial feature for enhancing user engagement in the RU AI Telegram funnel. Both commands are gated behind a confirmation flag to ensure user safety, preventing any unintended actions. The documentation has been updated to reflect these changes, and all API calls have been verified against official Telethon documentation.

items.title: Restrict App Download Page to Official Site in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The app download page is now exclusively displayed on the official main site, enhancing user experience by preventing access from subdomains and white-label domains. Key components like the header, footer, and user center have been updated to include checks for the main official host, ensuring that users are redirected appropriately. This change improves site integrity and reduces confusion for users accessing the download page.

items.title: Enhance Geo Detection for China in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces offline IP detection for mainland China in the `GET /config` endpoint, allowing the frontend to hide specific features for users from Chinese IPs. The response now includes a `country` field indicating the user's location, which can be expanded for future regions. Key changes include the addition of a CIDR list for China and improvements to the geo detection logic, ensuring efficient lookups without external calls.

items.title: Enhance Sub-site Branding for Recharge Titles in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update replaces the hardcoded 'Ace Data Cloud' in the global recharge title with a dynamic brand name placeholder across 18 languages. The changes ensure that sub-sites display their own brand names, improving brand consistency. The main site remains unaffected, continuing to show 'Ace Data Cloud' as before.

items.title: Enhance tgstat render efficiency in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a wall-time budget for tgstat renders, ensuring that retries do not exceed the agent sandbox's time limits. Key changes include a shared render deadline and adjustments to timeout settings, which help prevent wasted agent turns on stubborn pages. The implementation has been validated with successful lookups and passing unit tests.

items.title: Enhance mobile login with postMessage for iframe in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves mobile login issues by modifying the deep link handling in iframes. The `consumeNativeDeepLink` function now returns null for in-app iframes, ensuring that the login process falls back to using `postMessage`. This change unifies the login experience across mobile and desktop platforms, improving reliability and user experience.

items.title: Enhance Support Contacts Feature in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature allowing site owners to publish customer-service contacts on the About tab, which can be edited in Site Settings. The design is now extensible, enabling multiple contact types and custom entries without code changes. Additionally, the About tab will display specific first-party items only on official builds, enhancing user experience and support accessibility.

items.title: Enhance Customer Service Contacts in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `contacts` list to `Site.branding`, allowing for multiple customer service channels on Nexior's About tab. The schema supports various types like phone, email, and custom channels, ensuring flexibility without requiring schema changes. Comprehensive validation tests confirm the robustness of the implementation.

items.title: Exciting New Interaction Design Tokens in Common Frontend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a range of new design tokens, including control density, focus outlines, elevation, and motion tokens. The shared styles have been documented and validated through comprehensive testing. Additionally, the core package version has been bumped to 0.14.0, paving the way for future consumer adoption.

items.title: Center Dropdown Hover Highlight in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update fixes the vertical alignment issue in the service catalog dropdown within the site services override dialog. By making the dropdown items flex containers and adjusting padding, the hover highlight is now properly centered, enhancing the user interface. The change is purely CSS-based and has been validated with lint-staged checks.

items.title: Streamline Tailwind Theme with Semantic Tokens in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update removes the dormant purple Tailwind theme and replaces it with runtime semantic design tokens. Stale blue-purple surfaces and fixed purple glows have also been updated to use shared CSS variables. A regression test has been added to ensure the legacy palette does not return, closing a significant global-baseline gap.

items.title: Integrate TGSTAT_RENDER_TOKEN for Enhanced Skill Functionality in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update wires the TGSTAT_RENDER_TOKEN into the auth-backend pod environment, enabling the tgstat skill to render through WebExtractor. Key changes include modifications to the deployment YAML and the creation of a new secret for the token, ensuring that the system remains operational even if the token is not set. This enhancement improves the overall functionality and reliability of the skill.

items.title: Refactor to Use Shared Core Debug Utility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update upgrades the @acedatacloud/core package to version 0.13.0 and imports the debug utility from the shared core utils. The local log utility has been removed to eliminate duplication, streamlining the codebase. Validation steps confirm that the SSG build completes successfully without any stale imports or exports.

items.title: Refactor to Use Shared Core Debug Utility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update upgrades the @acedatacloud/core package to version 0.13.0 and imports the debug utility from the core utils. The local log utility has been removed to eliminate duplication, streamlining the codebase. Validation steps confirm that all builds and linting processes are successful, ensuring stability.

items.title: Fix login iframe CSP for native and desktop apps in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a white screen issue encountered when logging in via avatar taps on Nexior's native and desktop applications. By statically allowlisting the necessary app origins in the nginx configuration, the iframe can now render correctly, ensuring a seamless login experience across platforms. The changes also maintain compatibility with existing web domains.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Publish Flow in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the trusted publish flow for Xiaohongshu by implementing a real native button click within the closed shadow DOM using trusted CDP mouse events. Key changes include a refined event queue, better handling of publish responses, and enhanced error management. The validation suite has passed all tests, ensuring stability and reliability in the publishing process.

items.title: Enhance Dark Mode Readability in Nexior 🌙
items.summary: This update improves the visibility of the admin-only notice banner in the Settings section for dark mode users. By assigning explicit dark text colors to various tones, the text now contrasts effectively against the light banner background, ensuring readability across themes. This is a straightforward CSS adjustment with no logic changes.

items.title: Enhance tgstat Skill with Platform Token Injection in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the injection of a platform-held AceDataCloud API token into the `tgstat` skill sandbox, enabling it to render public tgstat.com pages via WebExtrator. Key changes include the addition of the `TGSTAT_RENDER_TOKEN` in the connector's metadata and a new test assertion to verify this integration. The deployment requires setting the token in the auth-backend secret for proper functionality.

items.title: Enhance tgstat Skill with WebExtrator for Cloudflare Bypass in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update reroutes tgstat page fetching through the WebExtrator service to bypass Cloudflare's restrictions, resolving previous HTTP 403 errors. Key changes include a new fetch layer, improved metric parsing, and enhanced recognition logic to ensure accurate data retrieval. The skill now supports retries for blocked requests and provides clear error handling for not-found scenarios.

items.title: Enhance Debug Utility in Common Frontend 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a duplicate router debug utility to the core utilities, ensuring the console receiver is preserved through a typed forwarding wrapper. The package version has been bumped to 0.13.0, and comprehensive tests have been added to validate functionality. Overall, this improves debugging capabilities for developers working with the Common Frontend.

items.title: Enhance Auth Status with Progress Reporting in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a visible text feature for the authentication status API, allowing users to see the login progress while WeChat is signing in. Key changes include the addition of a `progress` field that reports loading percentages and a standardized `page` identifier. The implementation ensures real-time action detection and improves error handling during the login process.
